title: Dim an LED with a Potentiometer
description: Adjust LED brightness using a potentiometer and an Arduino.

dialogue.text: Orion here again! Ready to add some interactivity? We'll twist a potentiometer to control an LED. Work on a static-safe, non-conductive surface and keep the Arduino unplugged so nothing shorts while you build.
Gather an Arduino Uno, a breadboard, three jumper wires, a 5 mm LED, a 220 Ω resistor, a potentiometer, a USB cable, and a laptop with the Arduino IDE. Keep components organized so leads don't touch.

Disconnect the USB cable. Set the potentiometer on the breadboard with its flat side facing you. Wire the outer legs to 5 V and GND, run the middle wiper to A0, and place the LED on pin 9 through the 220 Ω resistor so it survives. Use jumper wires, avoid crossing power rails, and double-check for shorts before powering up.

Reconnect the USB cable and open the Arduino IDE. Load the 'AnalogInOutSerial' example; it reads analogRead(A0), maps 0‑1023 to 0‑255, then analogWrite(9, value) to fade the LED. Select the correct port, upload, and avoid staring directly into the LED.
Nice work! Potentiometers give smooth control over voltage. Unplug the board before making changes, coil the USB cable, and store the parts for your next build.
